summary: Matched a large portion of melee/lb/lbcardnew and refined the neighboring lb_0192/lbcardnew split based on data and BSS access patterns. The PR moved several already-matched controller/timer functions from lbcardnew into lb_0192, added many concrete lbcardnew implementations around memory-card task dispatch and async CARD/HSD card operations, improved public/local headers and Dolphin SDK prototypes, and made lbcardnew functional enough to boot Melee in a nonmatching build. decomp.dev reported +6432 matched bytes, +0.17% matched code, 33 new matches, and 12 improved unmatched functions, with apparent broken matches explained by functions moving units due to the split correction.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/lb/lbcardnew.c from mostly placeholder declarations into many concrete functions, including lb_80019BB8, lb_80019C38, lb_80019CB0, lb_80019EF0, fn_8001A008, fn_8001A0B0, lb_8001A184, lb_8001A3A4, lb_8001A4CC, lb_8001A594, lb_8001A860, lb_8001A8A4, lb_8001A9CC, lb_8001AAE4, lb_8001AC04, lb_8001ACEC, lb_8001AE38, and later task/API wrapper functions through lb_8001C550.

Refined config/GALE01/splits.txt so lb_0192.c text extends from 0x80019230 to 0x80019BB8 instead of ending at 0x80019880, while lbcardnew.c starts at 0x80019BB8. The lb_0192 split gained BSS 0x804329F0-0x80432A68, sdata 0x804D3800-0x804D3808, and sdata2 0x804D7C80-0x804D7CB0; lbcardnew BSS was adjusted to start at 0x80432A68.
Moved controller/timer-related functions and the lb_804329F0 static state into src/melee/lb/lb_0192.c: lb_80019880, lb_80019894, lb_800198E0, lb_80019900, lb_80019A30, lb_80019A48, and lb_80019AAC.

Introduced or refined the lb_80432A68_t card-task/global-state structure in src/melee/lb/types.h, including work_area, lib_area, CARDFileInfo, task_array[11], callback field x50C, per-slot error/result arrays, and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x8B0).
Removed most structure definitions from lbcardnew.static.h and made it include melee/lb/types.h, leaving the lb_80432A68 static declaration local to lbcardnew.

Replaced many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS lbcardnew.h declarations with concrete signatures, const-correct string parameters, void returns for callbacks, and typed CardTask pointers where evidence supported them.
Added CARDFormatAsync to the public Dolphin card.h and removed its declaration from internal extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/card/__card.h.
Changed DVDGetCurrentDiskID prototype from an empty parameter list to DVDGetCurrentDiskID(void).

Added concrete sysdolphin/baselib prototypes in hsd_3AA7.h and hsd_3B27.h for HSD card helper calls used by lbcardnew, including hsd_803B24E4, hsd_803B2550, hsd_803B2674, hsd_803B27F4, hsd_803B286C, hsd_803B2928, hsd_803B29D8, hsd_803B2A4C, and hsd_803B2ADC.
smart_moves: Used data/BSS access patterns to correct the lb_0192/lbcardnew boundary instead of forcing functions into the old split; this preserved true unit ownership and explains decomp.dev's broken lbcardnew matches for functions that became lb_0192 matches.
Centralized the large lb_80432A68_t layout in melee/lb/types.h so lbcardnew and related headers can share a stable typed definition rather than duplicating guesses in lbcardnew.static.h.
Kept local static state local where appropriate: lb_804329F0_t and lb_804329F0 moved into lb_0192.c after the split refinement, while lb_80432A68 remains a static lbcardnew global.
Matched async CARD control flow with explicit OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts regions, callback decrementing of x8AC, and a sentinel return value 0xB for operations still pending.
Introduced small error-conversion helpers, especially lb_80019BB8 and convert_hsdcard_error, to normalize raw CARD/HSD-card return codes into lbcardnew's internal status values.
Modeled lbcardnew's operation queue as task_array[11] with task state 0xE as the free sentinel, and implemented reset_task_array plus lb_80019C38 allocation with an assertion when no slot exists.
Improved external SDK and baselib prototypes only where required by evidence from calls in the matched code, reducing reliance on placeholder macros and making future functions easier to type.
decomp_lessons: If a group of functions appears to match but accesses globals owned by a neighboring unit, revisit splits.txt before continuing; matched bytes can move between files when the underlying split is corrected.

Bot-reported broken matches are not always regressions: here seven lbcardnew functions became broken in lbcardnew because they were reclassified into lb_0192, while lb_0192 gained corresponding new matches.

For async memory-card code, track both the immediate API return code and the callback-completed count/state; matching may depend on preserving when x8AC is incremented/decremented and when interrupts are restored.
Use concrete callback prototypes such as void (*)(int, int) or CARDCallback once call sites establish them; replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS improves both matching and downstream header reliability.
Promote SDK functions to public headers when game code calls the public API directly, as with CARDFormatAsync in dolphin/card.h, rather than leaving the symbol only in an internal __card.h.

Large inferred globals should retain explicit offsets, padding, and STATIC_ASSERTs while still naming fields that are semantically clear, such as chan, work_area, lib_area, memsize, sectorsize, unused_bytes, unused_files, and task_array.
Nonmatching functional bootability can be a useful milestone for messy subsystems: the author explicitly notes lbcardnew was still messy but functional enough to boot Melee in a nonmatching build.
naming_conventions: Address-based symbols were retained for unknown game functions, e.g. lb_80019CB0, lb_8001A184, fn_8001A008, and fn_8001A0B0.
Static/global layout types used address-based names matching their backing symbols, such as lb_804329F0_t and lb_80432A68_t.

Unknown fields continued to use offset-like names such as x8AC, x50C, unk_34, unk_80, xD0, and xF4, while more obvious fields were named chan, work_area, lib_area, memsize, sectorsize, unused_bytes, unused_files, file_info, and task_array.
Task fields use compact offset-like names in struct CardTask, including x0, x4, x8, xC, x10, x18, and x19, reflecting incomplete semantic knowledge.
String pointer parameters were made const where call patterns supported it, such as const char* old_name, const char* new_name, and const char* filename-like arguments.
Header comments preserve address annotations beside prototypes, e.g. /* 019CB0 */ and /* 01A184 */, keeping declarations searchable by ROM address.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Corrected function ownership through split boundaries: lb_0192 text now ends at 0x80019BB8 and lbcardnew starts at 0x80019BB8, matching observed data/BSS access.
Used explicit local dummy stack arrays such as u8 _[8] in several functions, indicating stack-shape padding to satisfy matching constraints.
Used volatile stack temporaries in lb_8001ACEC with a TODO noting the code may be fake, showing a pragmatic matching workaround where the exact source shape was uncertain.
Preserved goto-based dispatch in lb_80019CB0 with an again label to process queued card tasks until an async-pending status 0xB or empty queue occurs.

Matched switch tables and status mappings for CARD/HSD-card error codes instead of flattening them, including special cases for -1, -2, -3, -4, -5, -6, -13, -14, -128 and HSD-card errors around -0x101 through -0x10B.
Used STATIC_ASSERTs on inferred structs, especially lb_80432A68_38_t size 0x8 and lb_80432A68_t size 0x8B0, to lock layout while continuing decomp work.
Matched asynchronous CARD operations with immediate status conversion, interrupt guards, x8AC pending-operation accounting, and callbacks fn_8001A008/fn_8001A0B0 updating global status fields.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says the split refinement was based on data/BSS access patterns and that the code remained messy but was functional enough to boot Melee in a nonmatching build.
decomp.dev reported matched code increased to 33.44%, a +0.17% gain and +6432 bytes, with 33 new matches.
decomp.dev listed 7 broken matches in lbcardnew, all for functions that the diff moved into lb_0192, suggesting the breakage was due to corrected unit ownership rather than lost matching work.
decomp.dev reported 12 improvements in still-unmatched lbcardnew functions, including lb_8001A594 at 95.53%, lb_8001C0F4 at 95.87%, lb_8001BA44 at 89.23%, and lb_8001B8C8 at 94.34%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or fully type lb_80432A68_t fields in lbcardnew.
Find later PRs completing the partially matched lbcardnew functions lb_8001A594, lb_8001C0F4, lb_8001BA44, lb_8001B8C8, lb_8001BE30, lb_8001BF04, lb_8001BC18, and lb_8001BB48.
Search for follow-up changes to the lb_0192/lbcardnew split boundaries or additional data/BSS ownership corrections.
Search for later cleanup of the TODO/volatile stack workaround in lb_8001ACEC.
Find PRs that improve names for CardTask states, lbcardnew internal status values, or CARD/HSD-card error mappings.
Search for further sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B27.h prototype refinements after this PR.
